title.rendered: AMI at Embedded World 2017 in Nuremberg, Germany: March 14-16, 2017

content.rendered:
NORCROSS, GEORGIA: – AMI, a global leader in BIOS and UEFI firmware, server and remote management tools, data storage products and unique solutions based on the Linux® and Android™ operating systems, is pleased to announce that it will once again participate at Embedded World 2017, happening from Tuesday, March 14 to Thursday, March 16, 2017 in Nuremberg, Germany.

The Embedded World Exhibition & Conference is considered the largest exhibition of its kind – where various members of the international embedded community come together and showcase developments in embedded technology. At Embedded World, attendees are presented with the latest trends in the embedded sector that span across software, hardware, tools and various development services and solutions.

This year, AMI will be located in Hall 2, Booth 2-200 in the Exhibition Centre Nuremberg, showcasing the latest developments in AMI’s Internet of Things (IoT) technologies – including vivIoT™, a new and easy-to-use cloud-based visual development tool for IoT firmware development. AMI engineers will also be on hand to discuss the most recent enhancements to AMI’s flagship Aptio® V UEFI BIOS firmware and the latest advanced embedded remote manageability solutions from MegaRAC®, AMI’s BMC division.

AMI invites its partners, customers, members of the press and exhibition guests to its booth at Embedded World, to learn more about these exciting developments and see the comprehensive end-to-end solutions the company has to offer the embedded controller, BIOS/UEFI, storage, BMC and remote management markets.

To learn more about Aptio V UEFI Firmware from AMI, please visit ami.com/products/aptio-v/.
